LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT OSL GROUP, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. PUNITIVE DAMAGES MAY BE AWARDED IN NEGLIGENCE CASES IF THE PLAINTIFF CAN ESTABLISH THAT THE DEFENDANT ENGAGED IN "DESPICABLE CONDUCT'WITH A WILLFUL AND CONSCIOUS DISREGARD OF THE RIGHTS OR SAFETY OF OTHERS." (CIV. CODE SEC. 3294(C)(1).) THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT PLEADS THAT DEFENDANT KNEW THAT CRIMINAL ACTIVITY, INCLUDING SEXUAL ASSAULTS, WAS OCCURRING IN THE WOODED AREA DURING THE FESTIVAL, BUT DEFENDANT TOOK NO ADDITIONAL PRECAUTIONS TO PROTECT ITS PATRONS. THIS IS SUFFICIENT TO SUPPORT A CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF STATES A CLAIM FOR DANGEROUS CONDITION OF PUBLIC PROPERTY UNDER GOV'T 835. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES THAT THE CONCERT VENUE WAS IN A DEFECTIVE CONDITION FOR INCLUDING A SECLUDED AND WOODED AREA KNOWN FOR HIGH CRIME WITHIN THE VENUE'S BOUNDARIES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: DENISE DOUCETTE, CSR #5963.

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-11-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bradley Bowles, Esq., 925.935.3300, for plaintiff Kaylee Ullom.
Robert Bragg, Esq., 415.273.6500, for defendant OSL Group, LLC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT OSL GROUP, LLC's MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is denied. Punitive damages may be awarded in negligence cases if the plaintiff can establish that the defendant engaged in "despicable conduct'with a willful and conscious disregard of the rights or safety of others." (Civ. Code sec. 3294(c)(1).) The Second Amended Complaint pleads that Defendant knew that criminal activity, including sexual assaults, was occurring in the wooded area during the festival, but Defendant took no additional precautions to protect its patrons. This is sufficient to support a claim for punitive damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.
